cards.back: A situation where two or more processes can access common resources simultaneously, but only one process can access a resource at a time.
The process of managing and regulating the interactions between different processes or threads in a distributed system.
A system that consists of multiple interconnected nodes or computers that work together to achieve a common goal.
A mutual consent or understanding between processes or threads in a distributed system to follow a specific protocol or behavior.
A protocol used to resolve conflicts in a distributed mutual exclusion situation, where one process passes a token to the next process in a queue.
